title: AssetRecipe
description:
A SEPAL Asset recipe (recipe id `ASSET_MOSAIC`). Wraps an Earth Engine `Image` or `ImageCollection` asset as a SEPAL recipe so it can be visualized, masked, filtered, composited, used as input to other recipes (classification, change detection, etc.), and exported through the standard pipeline.

**The two asset modes.** The recipe behaves differently depending on `assetDetails.type`:

- `'Image'` — the asset is a single image. The recipe applies `mask` (per-pixel band-value constraints) and clips to the AOI; `dates`, `filter`, and `composite` are ignored.
- `'ImageCollection'` — the asset is a collection. The recipe filters by AOI bounds, optionally by `dates`, optionally by image-property `filter`, applies `mask` per image, then reduces to a single image via `composite.type`.

**AOI semantics.** Unlike most recipes, the AOI may be `ASSET_BOUNDS` — meaning 'use the asset's own geometry'. This is the *only* recipe in SEPAL that supports the `ASSET_BOUNDS` AOI variant; for any other recipe, that variant is invalid.

This schema describes the persisted `model` of an *initialized* recipe. Cross-field constraints that JSON Schema cannot express are enforced by the companion rules.

aoi.description: Area of Interest. Asset is the only recipe that allows the `ASSET_BOUNDS` variant — meaning 'use the asset's intrinsic geometry'. EE_TABLE and POLYGON are also allowed for clipping the asset to a smaller area.

assetDetails.title: AssetDetails
assetDetails.description: The Earth Engine asset and its loaded metadata. The GUI populates this on asset selection: `assetId` is the user-supplied EE asset path, and `type`/`bands`/`visualizations`/`metadata` are loaded from EE.

assetId.description: Earth Engine asset path. Common forms: `users/<user>/<asset>`, `projects/<project>/assets/<asset>`, `<provider>/<dataset>/<image>` (catalog asset).

type.description: Asset type as reported by EE. `Image` recipes ignore `dates`, `filter`, and `composite`; only `ImageCollection` recipes use those fields.

bands.description: Per-band metadata loaded from EE. Each entry typically includes at least `id` (band name); EE may also include `data_type`, `crs`, `crs_transform`, `dimensions`. The LLM does not need to synthesize this; pull it from the loaded asset state.

visualizations.description: Visualization presets cached from the asset (categorical or continuous). Used by the Mask panel to detect categorical bands and surface their class values.

metadata.description: Full EE metadata blob (bands, properties, asset type, etc.). The Filter panel reads `metadata.properties` to expose filterable image-property names. Cosmetic for the LLM; populated by the GUI.

dates.title: AssetDates
dates.description:
Date filter applied to ImageCollections. **Ignored when `assetDetails.type='Image'`.** Three modes:

- `'ALL_DATES'` (default) — no date filter; every image in the collection is included.
- `'YEAR'` — calendar year filter; the GUI persists this as `fromDate=<year>-01-01` and `toDate=<year+1>-01-01` (the start-of-next-year toDate gives a full year inclusive of December via the `[fromDate, toDate)` half-open semantics).
- `'CUSTOM_DATE_RANGE'` — explicit `fromDate` / `toDate`. Same half-open semantics: scenes acquired ON `toDate` are NOT included.

fromDate.description: **Inclusive** start. Required when `type='YEAR'` or `type='CUSTOM_DATE_RANGE'`. Earliest valid date is `1970-01-01` (the GUI's lower bound on the date picker).

toDate.description: **Exclusive** end. Required when `type='YEAR'` or `type='CUSTOM_DATE_RANGE'`. Scenes acquired ON `toDate` are NOT included — set to the day AFTER the last day you want covered. For the full year 2024 use `2025-01-01`, NOT `2024-12-31`. Latest valid date is today.

allOf.description: fromDate and toDate required when type is YEAR or CUSTOM_DATE_RANGE.

composite.title: AssetComposite
composite.description: How an ImageCollection is reduced to a single image. **Ignored when `assetDetails.type='Image'`.**

type.description:
Reduction operator.

- `MOSAIC` (default) — last-image-wins per-pixel; preserves a single observation per pixel and keeps original values intact.
- `MEDIAN` — per-band per-pixel median across the collection. Robust to outliers; standard choice for clean composites.
- `MEAN` — per-band per-pixel arithmetic mean. Sensitive to outliers; rarely the best choice for noisy data.
- `MIN` / `MAX` — per-band per-pixel minimum / maximum. Useful for extreme-value compositing (e.g. minimum-cloudiness, maximum-greenness).
- `SD` — per-band per-pixel standard deviation across the collection. Output is variability rather than a representative value.
- `MODE` — per-band per-pixel mode (most-frequent value). Suitable for categorical bands (class labels) where averaging is meaningless.

filter.title: AssetFilter
filter.description:
Image-collection metadata filter applied per-image *before* compositing. **Ignored when `assetDetails.type='Image'`.** Each `filtersEntry` is a group of `constraints` combined with a `booleanOperator`. Multiple entries are combined with AND.

Note: filter constraints reference image *properties* (metadata fields like `CLOUD_COVER`, `system:time_start`), not image bands. Mask uses bands; filter uses properties — different operands.

mask.title: AssetMask
mask.description:
Per-pixel band-value mask applied to every image (Image asset) or every image in the collection (ImageCollection asset). Each `constraintsEntry` is a group of `constraints` combined with a `booleanOperator`. Multiple entries are combined with AND.

Unlike `filter` (which operates on metadata properties), `mask` operates on band *values*, supporting `class` and `range` operators plus optional bitwise extraction (`bit`, `fromBit`, `toBit`) for QA bitmask bands.

constraintsEntry.title: AssetConstraintsEntry
constraintsEntry.description: A group of constraints combined into a single boolean expression. Both `filter.filtersEntries[*]` and `mask.constraintsEntries[*]` use this shape; the per-constraint shape differs (filter uses `property`, mask uses `band`).

id.description: UUID for stable React keys and as the lookup path when the panel updates the entry.

booleanOperator.description: How the constraints are combined within this entry. `'and'` requires every constraint; `'or'` requires any.

constraint.title: AssetConstraint
constraint.description:
A single comparison. The shape varies by operator and by whether this constraint belongs to a `filter` (then it has `property`) or a `mask` (then it has `image` and `band`, and may have bit-extraction fields).

- Filter constraints: `{property, operator, value | range fields}` — `operator` ∈ `<`, `≤`, `>`, `≥`, `=`, `range`. Filters do NOT support `class` (image properties aren't categorical).
- Mask constraints: `{image, band, operator, value | range fields | selectedClasses}` plus optional `bit`/`fromBit`/`toBit`/`fromBitInclusive`/`toBitInclusive` for bitwise extraction. `operator` ∈ `class`, `<`, `≤`, `>`, `≥`, `=`, `range`.

`≤` and `≥` are unicode U+2264 / U+2265.

description.description: Human-readable summary shown in the GUI; not used by the backend.

image.description: Mask constraints only. Always the literal string `'this-recipe'` — masks reference bands of the asset itself.

band.description: Mask constraints only. Band name to read; must be one of `assetDetails.bands[*].id`.

property.description: Filter constraints only. Image-metadata property name (typically a numeric or string property like `CLOUD_COVER`, `LANDSAT_PRODUCT_ID`, `system:time_start`).

operator.description: Comparison operator. `class` is mask-only (categorical band match against `selectedClasses`). `range` selects values between `from` and `to` with inclusive flags. `=` is supported by both filter (string or numeric property equality) and mask (numeric band equality).

selectedClasses.description: Required when `operator='class'` (mask only). Pixel matches if its band value is any of these.

value.description: Right-hand-side value. Required for `<`, `≤`, `>`, `≥`, `=`. Numeric for mask; numeric or string for filter (depending on the property's type).

from.description: Lower bound for `range`. Required for `range` operator only.

to.description: Upper bound for `range`. Required for `range` operator only.

fromInclusive.description: Whether the lower bound is inclusive. Required for `range` operator only.

toInclusive.description: Whether the upper bound is inclusive. Required for `range` operator only.

bit.description: Mask constraints only. When `true`, the comparison runs on a bitwise extraction of the band value rather than the raw value. Useful for QA bitmask bands (e.g. Landsat `QA_PIXEL`). Requires `fromBit`/`toBit`/`fromBitInclusive`/`toBitInclusive`.

fromBitInclusive.description: Whether `fromBit` is inclusive in the extracted range. Used when `bit=true`.

toBitInclusive.description: Whether `toBit` is inclusive in the extracted range. Used when `bit=true`.

allOf.description: Range operator requires from, to, fromInclusive, toInclusive.
bit=true requires fromBit, toBit, and inclusive flags.
